name: Serra da Estrela: Manteigas and Torre Loop
name_original: Da Manteigas verso il lago Comprida – Parco Naturale della Serra da Estrela
description: This difficult 37.8-mile mountain biking loop in Serra da Estrela offers panoramic views from Torre and passes the Lagoa Comprida Dam.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Manteigas (Santa Maria) traverse the dramatic glacial landscapes of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. The region is characterized by the iconic "U"-shaped Zêzere Glacier Valley, offering diverse terrain from rocky paths to forested areas and open meadows. Mountain bikers can expect significant elevation changes and challenging routes through this geologically rich environment. The area provides a remarkable setting for outdoor enthusiasts seeking robust mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Manteigas (Santa Maria) The most popular mountain bike trail is Serra da Estrela: Manteigas and Torre Loop, a 37.8 miles (60.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive climbs and descents through the high mountain landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Loop from Manteigas to Covão da Ponte – Serra da Estrela Natural Park, a difficult 29.4 miles (47.3 km) path. This route explores the natural park, passing through varied terrain and offering panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Great Zêzere Route, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through the Zêzere Glacier Valley, often completed in about 4 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Manteigas (Santa Maria) is defined by glacial valleys, granite formations, and extensive forested areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from intermediate to very difficult trails. The routes in Manteigas (Santa Maria) are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 70 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Manteigas (Santa Maria)'s varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Manteigas (Santa Maria)?
A: There are nearly 10 mountain bike trails around Manteigas (Santa Maria) listed on komoot, offering a range of experiences through the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Manteigas (Santa Maria) traverse dramatic glacial landscapes, featuring diverse terrain from rocky paths to forested areas and open meadows. You can expect significant elevation changes, with routes classified from moderate to difficult. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330365737/serra-da-estrela-manteigas-and-torre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra da Estrela: Manteigas and Torre Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 60.8 km trail with extensive climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easier mountain bike trails in the area?
A: While many routes in Manteigas are classified as difficult, the region's dedicated Mountain Bike Center offers trails categorized by difficulty, including Green (Easy, for beginners) and Blue (Intermediate, for regular riders). These easier options are suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride, often featuring circular paths like the 'Rota do Sameiro' (Blue) and 'Rota da Lapa' (Green) mentioned in regional information.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike routes?
A: The trails offer access to stunning natural features. You can encounter the iconic "U"-shaped Zêzere Glacier Valley, with breathtaking panoramic views such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4416223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Zêzere Glacial Valley&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sights include the captivating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6019826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hidden cascade at Poço do Inferno&lt;/a&gt;, which can even freeze in winter, and the picturesque Covão d'Ametade with its meandering Zêzere river framed by birch trees.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330306349/loop-from-manteigas-to-covao-da-ponte-serra-da-estrela-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop from Manteigas to Covão da Ponte – Serra da Estrela Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 47.3 km path exploring varied terrain. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330546132/folgosinho-and-videmonte-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgosinho and Videmonte Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 44.8 km ride.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Manteigas (Santa Maria)?
A: Manteigas offers diverse conditions throughout the year. While the region is beautiful in autumn with the vibrant colors of Faias Wood, and Poço do Inferno can freeze in colder winters, the best time for mountain biking generally depends on your preference for weather and trail conditions. Spring and early autumn often provide pleasant temperatures and stable conditions for riding.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region is home to several significant water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4915608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Long Lake&lt;/a&gt; (Lagoa Comprida), the largest water reservoir in Serra da Estrela, is a prominent feature. You might also encounter the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/374228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covão dos Conchos Trail&lt;/a&gt;, known for its bell-mouth spillway, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3477370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vale do Rossim Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Absolutely. Manteigas is well-suited for experienced riders, with 6 out of the 9 listed komoot routes classified as difficult. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330285159/great-zezere-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Zêzere Route&lt;/a&gt;, a 47.3 km trail through the Zêzere Glacier Valley, offer significant elevation gains and technical sections. The region's diverse terrain, including rocky paths and steep descents, caters to advanced riding styles.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Manteigas (Santa Maria)?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Mountain bikers frequently praise the dramatic glacial landscapes, the challenging nature of the routes, and the stunning panoramic views, particularly those found along the Zêzere Glacier Valley. The variety of terrain and the immersive natural experience are also often highlighted.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the Zêzere Glacier Valley?
A: Yes, the Zêzere Glacier Valley is a central feature of the region's mountain biking. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330285159/great-zezere-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Zêzere Route&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading directly through this iconic valley. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2330590394/manteigas-and-zezere-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manteigas and Zêzere Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 35 km ride showcasing the valley's beauty.
tips:
It is considered the "capital of Serra da Estrela" and a privileged starting point to explore the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. 

Manteigas is famous for its wool textile industry, especially for the production of fabrics and blankets. Manteigas is also known for its natural beauty, with glacial valleys (such as Zêzere), waterfalls and unique rock formations, including the famous Cântaro Magro.
Gravel road between Unhais da Serra and Nave de Santo António. Quiet traffic-free climbing.

Lagoa Comprida is the largest glacial lake in Serra da Estrela, Portugal, and one of the most iconic spots in the Natural Park. Its name, meaning "Long Lake," perfectly describes its elongated shape, nestled between the surrounding peaks.

Originally a glacial lake, it was artificially enlarged with the construction of a dam in the early 20th century for hydroelectric power generation, becoming an important reservoir. Its crystalline waters reflect the sky and the rugged surrounding mountains, creating breathtaking views. It is a popular starting point for hikes and treks to other lagoons and peaks, and offers a sense of grandeur and serenity typical of alpine environments.

faqs.question: Where does the Serra da Estrela: Manteigas and Torre Loop start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The loop typically starts from Manteigas, which is recognized as a mountain bike center and an excellent starting point for rides towards Torre. Parking is generally available in Manteigas, a picturesque village nestled in the Zêzere Glacial Valley.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and challenging, featuring rugged sections, rocky slopes, dense forests, and open meadows. You'll encounter a mix of paved roads, fire roads, and singletrack. The landscape is characterized by dramatic granite formations and glacial valleys.

faqs.question: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience stunning alpine-like scenery with panoramic views, deep ravines, and cold mountain streams. Notable features include the village of Manteigas, the View of Unhais da Serra, and the Lagoa Comprida Dam. The ascent to Torre, mainland Portugal's highest point, offers breathtaking vistas across central Portugal.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this loop is generally considered difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It requires a very good level of fitness due to significant elevation gain, particularly the ascent to Torre. Some strenuous segments may even require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are ideal. In spring, wildflowers add vibrant color to the rocky areas. Autumn offers crisp air and beautiful light. Summer can be very hot, and winter may bring snow and challenging conditions at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed in Serra da Estrela Natural Park, it's advisable to keep them on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife encounters. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a challenging, long ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in Serra da Estrela Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on this route within the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with official routes such as [CIMA PR07] Torre * Manteigas, Rota do Glaciar, [CIMA PR07] Torre * San Romao, and Grande Rota do Zêzere, among others.

faqs.question: Is navigation straightforward on the trail?
faqs.answer: The extensive network of trails in the region can sometimes be inconsistently signposted. For reliable navigation, using a GPS device with the route downloaded or hiring a local guide is highly recommended to stay on track.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, Manteigas serves as an excellent base with various amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants offering local cuisine (like the renowned Queijo da Serra cheese), and accommodation options before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What are the main challenges on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The primary challenge is the significant and sustained ascent to Torre, the highest point in mainland Portugal. The varied terrain, including rocky sections and steep climbs, demands strong technical skills and endurance. Be prepared for a long day in the saddle.
